title: rba / reserve bank australia / cash rate | Central Bank Rate Decision | Statement by the Monetary Policy Board: Monetary Policy Deci...
summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia raised its cash rate target by 25 basis points to 4.10 percent, citing a material pickup in inflation during the second half of 2025 and upside risks to the inflation outlook. The Board noted that capacity pressures have increased, short-term inflation expectations have risen, and the Middle East conflict has led to sharply higher fuel prices. The decision was made by a majority vote of five members, with four members preferring to leave rates unchanged at 3.85 percent.
